description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1529 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0559#AN1538 . The description of the approach follows: Correlate process execution of shutdown/reboot commands (e.g., shutdown.exe, restart-computer) with host status change logs (Event IDs 1074, 6006) and absence of related administrative context (e.g., user not in Helpdesk group).

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1529: System Shutdown/Reboot

items.content: Identify and monitor for the execution of shutdown or reboot commands on Windows systems, specifically targeting processes like shutdown.exe, restart-computer, and other similar utilities.

Collect and analyze Windows Event Logs for system shutdown/restart events, focusing on Event ID 1074 (indicating a process initiated a shutdown) and Event ID 6006 (indicating a clean shutdown event was logged).
Correlate the process execution events with the system event logs to identify matches where a shutdown command execution is followed by a corresponding shutdown event (e.g., Event ID 1074 or 6006).
Verify the user account that executed the shutdown command is not a member of authorized administrative groups (e.g., Helpdesk, Administrators, or other designated groups with shutdown privileges).
Review the reason code and comment in Event ID 1074 for legitimacy; suspicious entries may include unexpected reasons or lack of explanation.
Cross-reference the shutdown time with known maintenance windows or scheduled tasks to rule out planned activities.
Check for the absence of administrative context or legitimate authorization for the shutdown/reboot action.

If correlation indicates an unauthorized or suspicious shutdown/reboot (e.g., executed by a non-privileged user, outside maintenance hours, with no valid reason), escalate the event for further investigation as a potential security incident.
Document all findings, including the user, process, command-line arguments, event log details, and correlation results, for incident response and reporting.
